assertions.assertion: Response identifies ASP.NET Core Data Protection as the root cause of the decryption failure
assertions.rationale: Clearly identifies 'ASP.NET Core Data Protection' as the root cause in the first paragraph.

assertions.assertion: Response explains that the default application name (content root path discriminator) changed between .NET versions
assertions.rationale: Detailed explanation of how the default application name is derived from content root path and how it changed between .NET versions.

assertions.assertion: Response recommends calling SetApplicationName() on AddDataProtection() with an explicit application name
Response mentions ensuring old Data Protection keys are still accessible (not deleted or rotated away)
assertions.rationale: Dedicated section 'Ensure Old Keys Are Still Accessible' with three specific scenarios.
